{"step_by_step_thinking": "H.S. Purpura (Henoch-Schönlein purpura) is known to cause palpable purpura, which is a key feature of the condition. Mixed cryoglobulinemia can also lead to palpable purpura, as it is associated with immune complex deposition in small blood vessels. Drug-induced vasculitis can cause palpable purpura as well, due to the immune response triggered by the drug. However, giant cell arteritis typically presents with tender scalp tenderness, jaw claudication, and temporal artery tenderness, rather than palpable purpura.", "answer_choice": "C"}
